objective: Create the ABYS validator task for Authalien candidate records so future A2A and M2M SYNTEL payloads can reference bounded ITEM artifacts instead of decorative language.
assumptions: ITEM owns Authalien as a candidate machine-native symbolic system.
ABYS owns validation tasks and tests, not Authalien canon meaning.
SYNTEL may carry Authalien only as signed, auditable, replayable A2A/M2M payload data after validation.

routing_decision.reason: This packet creates the validator gate between ITEM candidate records and future SYNTEL A2A/M2M payload extensions.

files.notes: Validate ITEM Authalien candidate records and route decisions.
Invalid fixture proving decorative or unbounded glyph language is rejected.

implementation_steps: Load the ITEM candidate record from candidates/authalien-primal-glyph-set.json.
Assert the record is status candidate and object_type machine_native_symbolic_system.
Validate that future glyph inventory fixtures include bounded symbol ids, names, meanings, and transformation rules.
Assert routeTask keeps Codex validator work in ABYS with ITEM handoff rather than SYNTEL auto-routing.
Document that SYNTEL may only carry validated Authalien payload references inside signed envelopes.
risks_blockers: Authalien can become decorative protocol slop if SYNTEL receives it before ITEM and ABYS validation gates are explicit.
A2A/M2M payloads must remain auditable and replayable rather than autonomous by default.
next_recursive_improvement: After the validator exists, add SYNTEL conformance examples for signed envelopes carrying validated Authalien payload references.
routing_notes: This packet intentionally prepares A2A/M2M Authalien use without moving canon meaning or runtime authority out of ITEM and ABYS gates.
